title: Co-designing Nova Sleepcare: identifying priority areas for implementation.
abstract: Sleep problems are common in youth with neurodevelopmental disorders and directly contribute to behaviors of concern. While behavioral sleep education improves both issues, a disconnect exists between research and clinical reality: evidence-based sleep interventions are mainly delivered through specialized clinics and multi-week programs, which are often inaccessible to families needing immediate support. To bridge this gap we co-designed with end-users, a physician-delivered, point-of-care active sleep education intervention called Nova Sleepcare that can be directly implemented in clinical and household setting.
